note:
1989年8月24日是许多棒球迷声名狼藉的日子。那一天，皮特·罗斯被永久取消了进入棒球名人堂投票的资格。2020年4月23日是我自己声名狼藉的日子。这一天，我也被Quora终身禁赛。
是什么违规行为让我被禁赛？我说地球不是平的。
Quora是一个问答网站，任何人都可以在这里提问，任何人都可以回答。它把问题货币化，所以有一种动机，就是在无数不同的变种中问一些愚蠢而空洞的问题。我看到的这些Quora问题的一些变体包括：
用&lt；Language&gt；怎么说&lt；Term&gt；？-数百人选择先不检查谷歌翻译。
网站是骗局吗？-有数十亿个域名，这里有很多问题。
当前发生的任何事件都是阴谋吗？-如果你问Quora，很有可能是。
在Quora上，我更像是一个被动的用户，每天都会收到关于我关注的主题的电子邮件摘要。几个月前，我开始回答关于地球平坦的问题。为什么？我不知道。我不能不回复，所以我做了我觉得必须做的事情。
他们的增长在一定程度上与人们对科学和政府的总体怀疑有关，再加上在这个话题上可以赚钱的事实特别是在YouTube上，那里的平地频道往往有很多订阅者。随着YouTube(和Quora一样)将内容货币化，观众越多，平坦的地球人赚的钱就越多。
在YouTube上，平坦的地球人制作可爱而戏剧性的视频，通常伴随着戏剧性的音乐。他们发现了一个科学上的进退两难的问题，因为他们不能回答这个问题，他们将其归咎于NASA参与了一个阴谋，目的是让人们不知道地球是平的。
我承认，我最初对平坦地球人的一些回应违反了Quora的“友善，尊重”原则，该原则要求人们以礼貌、尊重和体贴的态度对待网站上的其他人。我尊重反对或不同的观点和信仰(如果有证据支持结论，甚至是不同的结论)，但当一些人不能忠于科学和常识时，很难忠于这些原则。
长话短说，我叫几个人白痴，还有几个人叫白痴。我得到了警告，承认了这一点，不再把那些相信地球是平的人称为白痴和白痴。
在我的警告之后，我是一个优秀的Quora用户。我很有礼貌，甚至对那些完全无视科学的人也是如此，而且我注意自己的语言。不要误会我的意思-我仍然和平坦的地球人互动，我仍然告诉他们他们错了。我只是试图不违反Quora的政策和指导方针，因为我这样做了。
但是我的回答似乎激怒了平坦的地球人，不管我多么有礼貌。
当我引用我自己的文章时，似乎把他们逼到了边缘，我在文章中写道，有一次，平坦的地球人模仿了一次长途飞行，使用的是平坦的地球航线，他们认为他们的航班应该比“环地”飞机早几个小时到达那里。
从科学的角度来看，从来没有平坦的地球人给过我一个理性的回答，为什么它是不正确的。因此，他们没有回答科学领域的问题，而是将我的回答报告为垃圾信息。
彼得·塔特福德(Peter Tatford)认为，像我这样的答案经常被阴谋论者标记出来，他们发布了荒谬的问题，然后又不同意他们收到的答案。恶意报道一直是Quora上的一个持续存在的问题，但除了呼吁并继续与正义作斗争之外，任何人都无能为力。
另一人写道，他也受到了Quora的审核团队的骚扰。他写道：
显然，Quora的节制系统已经疯了，现在的行为就像一只疯狗。就像疯狗咬任何稍微惹恼它们的人一样，Quora的温和系统也是这样做的。他们删除了我的7个答案(因为违反了模糊定义的“要友善，要尊重”的政策)，删除了8条评论(其中一些只是简单的评论，比如“哦，我不知道”或“你为什么要问”，因为同样的原因也被删除了)。因此，我不得不匿名回答，因为我不能冒着因为愚蠢的原因而被禁止进入我最喜欢的网站的风险。
Quora的大部分都是由机器人运营的，这使得上诉变得相当困难。我确实试图上诉，但得到的回复很好，模棱两可，模板化，说我仍然被禁止。
虽然我的回答最初可能违反了“友善，尊重”的原则，但Quora让平坦的地球人和其他真正错误的人可以自由支配。我想我们短期内不会看到“要科学，避免毫无根据的阴谋论”的原则。
note_en:
August 24, 1989 is a day of infamy for many baseball fans. That is the day Pete Rose was made permanently ineligible from being placed on the Baseball Hall of Fame ballot. April 23, 2020 was my very own day of infamy. It was the day that I, too, got a lifetime ban — from Quora.
  What was the trespass that got me banned?  I said that the earth is not flat.
   Quora is a question and answer website where anyone can ask a question, and anyone can answer. It monetizes questions, so there is somewhat of an incentive to ask silly and inane questions in a myriad of different variants. Some variants of these Quora questions I have seen are:
 ● How do you say <term> in <language>? — Hundreds of people have chosen not to check Google Translate first.
 ● Is <website> a scam? — With billions of domain names, there are lots of questions here.
 ● Is <any current event> a conspiracy?  — It very well could be, if you ask Quora.
  I was more of a passive user with Quora, getting a daily email digest on topics I followed. A few months back, I started replying to the flat earth questions. Why? I don’t know. I couldn’t NOT reply, so I did what I felt I had to do.
  Part of their growth is related to the overall skepticism people have in science and government, combined with the fact that there’s money to be made on the topic — especially on YouTube, where flat earth channels tend to have a lot of subscribers. As YouTube (like Quora) monetizes content, the more viewers, the more money flat earthers make.
 On YouTube, flat earthers make cute and dramatic videos, often with melodramatic music. They find a scientific quandary, and since they can’t answer it, they chalk it up to NASA being part of a conspiracy to keep the people from knowing that the earth is flat.
  I’ll admit, some of my initial responses to flat earthers violated the Quora “be nice, be respectful” principle, which requires that people treat other people on the site with civility, respect, and consideration. I’m all about respecting opposing or differing opinions and beliefs (and even differing conclusions if there’s some evidence to support the conclusion), but it’s difficult to stay true to these principles when some people can’t stay true to science and common sense.
 Long story short, I called a few people idiots and a few others morons. I got a warning about that, acknowledged it, and stopped referring to the people who believe the earth is flat as idiots and morons.
 After my warning, I was an excellent Quora user. I was courteous, even to people who completely disregard science, and I watched my language. Don’t get me wrong — I still interacted with flat earthers and I still told them they were wrong. I just sought not to violate the Quora Policies and Guidelines as I did it.
 But it seems as if my answers angered the flat earthers, no matter how courteous I was.
 What seemed to drive them off the edge was when I referenced  my own article, where I wrote about the time that flat-earthers mimicked a long-haul flight using a flat earth route, believing that their flight should get there hours before the “round earth” plane.
 From a scientific perspective, no flat earther has ever given me a rational response to why it is incorrect. So rather than answer the field of science, they reported my answers as spam.
  Peter Tatford  suggested that answers such as mine are often flagged by conspiracy theorists who post ridiculous questions and then disagree with the answers they receive. Malicious reporting has been an ongoing problem on Quora, but there’s not much anyone can do except make an appeal and carry on fighting the good fight.
 Another person  wrote that he has also been harassed by Quora’s moderation team. He writes:
 apparently, Quora’s moderation system has gone insane and is now acting like a rabid dog. And just like rabid dogs bite anyone who slightly annoys them, Quora’s moderation system does the same. They’ve collapsed 7 of my answers (for violating the vaguely-defined “ be nice, be respectful” policy) and removed eight comments (some of which are just simple comments like “ Oh, I didn’t know” or “ why do you ask,” which get removed for the same reasons). As a result, I have to answer using anonymity because I can’t risk getting banned from my favorite website for dumb reasons”.
 Much of Quora is run by bots, which makes appealing pretty difficult. I did try to appeal and I got a nice, vague, templated response that that I am still banned.
  While my answers may have initially violated the “be nice, be respectful” principle, Quora lets flat earthers and other people who are literally wrong have free reign. I guess we won’t be seeing a “be scientific, avoid baseless conspiracy theories” principle anytime soon.
title: Quora禁止我说地球不是平的
title_en: Quora Banned Me for Saying the Earth Isn't Flat

note:
这些天来，美国汽车亲英症似乎主要集中在路虎卫士(Land Rover Defender)上。这些功利进口的二手车要价可与类似农用的G-wagen相媲美，但没有嘻哈视频明星的外表，远远超过你在原产国购买一辆破旧农用车的价格。一个新的后卫即将问世，它更加现代化，甚至应该及时带来一些电气化，但如果你想在不消耗任何汽油的情况下驾驶一辆方方正正的越野赛车，那主要是在等待布林格(Bollinger)。但现在有了另一种选择，尽管它更加昂贵：Twisted NAS-E。
它是Twisted Automotive的产品，Twisted Automotive是一家总部位于约克郡的公司，存在的目的是重塑捍卫者，除了捍卫者什么都不是。和Singer和Porsches一样，Twisted找到了老兰迪，并将它们装饰起来，增加了现代动力总成和奢华的皮革包裹内饰。这主要是指使用通用LT1或LT4V8，但现在也有电动选项。
NAS-E一开始是一款排序轴距软顶防御器90，但它不是小块V8，而是由160千瓦(214马力)和380 Nm(280磅英尺)的人头马达提供动力。反过来，这台马达由一个60kWh的电池组供电，Twisted表示，这将提供200英里(321公里)的续航里程。我不完全确定Twisted的电池是从哪里来的--我们已经询问了该公司，如果我们发现了，我会更新这篇帖子。
该动力总成与四轮驱动减速直接变速器相耦合，带有一个双速变速箱。悬架使用了奇特的越野减震器，所有四个车轮上都有大刹车，前部有六个活塞卡钳，后部有四个活塞卡钳。
此外，你会得到一个比路虎安装过的更好的内饰，加上一个信息娱乐系统，空调和滚动笼。然而，它不会便宜--NAS-E起价18.5万美元。或者，花21万美元，你可以购买NAS-E Plus，它拥有更强大的238kW(320马力)、420 Nm(309磅英尺)的电动机和一些额外的车身装饰品。
note_en:
American automotive anglophilia appears to focus mainly on the Land Rover Defender these days. Used examples of these utilitarian imports  command asking prices that rival the similarly agricultural G-wagen but without the veneer of hip-hop video stardom, far outstripping what you'd pay for an old and battered farm vehicle in its country of origin.  A new Defender is on the way, one that's much more modern and should even come with some electrification in time, but if you want to drive something boxy off-road without burning any gas, that's mostly meant waiting for a  Bollinger. But now there's an alternative, albeit one that's  even more expensive: the Twisted NAS-E.
 It's the product of Twisted Automotive, a Yorkshire-based company that exists to restomod Defenders, and nothing but Defenders. Like Singer and Porsches, Twisted finds old Landies and spiffs them up, adding a modern powertrain and luxurious leather-wrapped interior.  That's mostly meant using a GM LT1 or LT4 V8, but now there's also an electric option.
 The NAS-E starts off as a sort-wheelbase soft-top Defender 90, but instead of a small-block V8, it's powered by a Remy Borg-Warner electric motor with 160kW (214hp) and 380Nm (280lb-ft). That motor, in turn, is powered by a 60kWh battery pack, which Twisted says will provide 200 miles (321km) of range. I'm not entirely sure where Twisted is getting its batteries from—we've asked the company, and I'll update this post if we find out.
 That powertrain is coupled to a four-wheel-drive gear-reduction direct transmission, with a two-speed transfer case. The suspension uses fancy off-road dampers, and there are big brakes on all four wheels, with six-piston calipers for the front and four-piston calipers at the rear.
 In addition, you get a much nicer interior than Land Rover ever installed, plus an infotainment system, air conditioning, and a roll cage. However, it's not going to be cheap—the NAS-E starts at $185,000. Or, for $210,000, you could spring for the NAS-E Plus, which gets a more powerful 238kW (320hp), 420Nm (309lb-ft) electric motor and some extra body adornments.
title: 想要一辆由电池驱动的四四方方的SUV吗？了解扭曲的NAS-E
title_en: Want a boxy SUV that runs on batteries? Check out the Twisted NAS-E

note:
特朗普指责全球广播公司对政府及其政策过于批评，包括对冠状病毒的回应。帕克驱逐媒体领袖的行动被视为努力吸引更友好的报道。这些组织的资深人士表示，大规模的领导层更迭破坏了他们传统的独立性。
特朗普政府试图对纳税人资助的实体施加更大控制的努力吸引了国会的关注，国会对帕克的举动提出了质疑，同时引发了一场不同寻常的法律战。
本月早些时候，美国地区法官贝丽尔·豪厄尔(Beryl Howell)拒绝了OTF董事会现有成员提出的阻止帕克对他们采取行动的请求。
然而，在新的上诉法院命令中，华盛顿特区巡回法院的法官表示，帕克在专注于互联网的非营利组织方面似乎缺乏他对其他联邦资助的国际媒体组织享有的同样的权威。
“Otf不是广播商…。而且与[争议法律]中明确列出的广播实体不够相似，不符合法定文本，“法院的未签署命令说。Pack的“法定权威…”似乎不包括对OTF董事会或运营的控制。“。
豪厄尔发现，如果旧董事会被锁在门外，OTF不会受到不可弥补的损害，但由三名法官组成的上诉小组不同意这一观点。
“政府的行为已经危及了OTF与其伙伴组织的关系，导致其伙伴组织担心他们的安全，”华盛顿特区巡回法庭命令说。此外，如果在上诉过程中没有禁制令，OTF将面临越来越大的风险，即其决策将被政府接管，它将遭受声誉损害，并将失去根据目前存在的两个决斗委员会有效运作的能力。“。
上诉法院的命令是暂时的，并不等同于对帕克的权威做出最终裁决，但似乎是对他试图清理美国资助的媒体实体内部事务的第一次严重法律打击。周二发布的禁令将OTF的控制权归还给之前的董事会，直到上诉得到充分审理和裁决。
华盛顿特区巡回法庭命令发布的前一天，哥伦比亚特区总检察长卡尔·拉辛(Karl Racine)向华盛顿高级法院提起诉讼，声称试图收购OTF违反了华盛顿特区关于非营利组织治理的法律。
政府广播机构内部和内部的动荡已经开始影响到该基金的运作。上周五，Politico报道称，OTF可能需要取消一份加密软件的合同，该软件帮助生活在伊朗和中国等专制政权的人们在政府不知情的情况下获取公正的新闻。
在6月26日的法庭听证会上，司法部的一名律师表示，对OTF资金的冻结已经或即将解除。全球媒体机构的一名官员周二说，近一个月过去了，资金流还没有恢复。
周二发布禁令的上诉法官是克林顿任命的大卫·塔特尔(David Tatel)、乔治·W·布什(George W.Bush)任命的托马斯·格里菲斯(Thomas Griffith)和奥巴马任命的帕特里夏·米利特(Patricia Millett。上诉可能会被分配给另一个小组进行未来的诉讼。
古普塔说，在过去的几周里，帕克和他的经纪人试图为OTF安排一位代理首席执行官，试图进入OTF的办公场所，甚至试图迫使OTF的房东交出租约控制权。这不仅会伤害OTF和在那里工作的人。OTF致力于在世界各地的专制政权中推进互联网自由，而Pack的行动已经将德黑兰和香港等地的活动家和记者的安全置于危险之中。
古普塔补充说：我们希望帕克和政府能遵守上诉法院的命令。
司法部拒绝置评，而美国通用汽车发言人没有立即回复对新裁决的置评请求。
note_en:
Trump has taken the global broadcasters to task for being too critical of the administration and its policies, including its response to the coronavirus. Pack’s drive to oust the leaders of the media outlets was seen as an effort to draw friendlier coverage. Veterans of the organizations have said the massive leadership change undermined their traditional independence.
 The Trump administration’s attempts to exert greater control over the taxpayer-funded entities has drawn in Congress, which has raised questions about Pack’s moves, while setting off an unusual legal battle.
 Earlier this month, U.S. District Judge Beryl Howell  turned down a request by the existing members of the OTF board to block Pack’s move against them.
 However, in the  new appeals court order, the judges from the D.C. Circuit said Pack appeared to lack the same authority over the internet-focused nonprofit that he enjoys over the other federally funded international media organizations.
 “OTF is not a broadcaster … and is not sufficiently similar to the broadcast entities expressly listed in [the law at issue] to fit within the statutory text,” the court’s unsigned order said. Pack’s “statutory authority … does not seem to include control of OTF’s board or operations.”
 Howell found no irreparable harm would befall OTF if the old board was locked out, but the three-judge appeals panel disagreed.
 “The government’s actions have jeopardized OTF’s relationships with its partner organizations, leading its partner organizations to fear for their safety,” the D.C. Circuit order said. “Further, absent an injunction during the appellate process, OTF faces an increasing risk that its decision-making will be taken over by the government, that it will suffer reputational harm, and that it will lose the ability to effectively operate in light of the two dueling boards that presently exist.”
 The appeals court order is temporary and does not amount to a final ruling on Pack’s authority, but appears to be the first serious legal blow to his attempt to clean house at U.S.-funded media entities. The injunction issued Tuesday returns control of OTF to its previous board until the appeal is fully heard and decided.
 The D.C. Circuit order came one day after District of Columbia Attorney General Karl Racine filed his own lawsuit in D.C. Superior Court, claiming the attempted takeover of OTF violated D.C. law on governance of nonprofit organizations.
 The turmoil over and within the government’s broadcasting agency has begun to affect the fund’s operations. Last Friday, POLITICO  reported that OTF might need to cancel a contract for encryption software that helps people living in repressive regimes such as Iran and China access impartial news without their government’s knowledge.
 At a court hearing on June 26, a Justice Department lawyer indicated that a freeze on OTF's funding had been or was about to be lifted. An official with the Agency for Global Media said Tuesday that, nearly a month later, the funding stream has yet to resume.
 The appeals judges who issued the injunction Tuesday were Clinton appointee David Tatel, George W. Bush appointee Thomas Griffith and Obama appointee Patricia Millett. The appeal will likely be assigned to another panel for future proceedings.
   "Over the past few weeks, Pack and his agents have tried to install an 'Acting CEO' for OTF, have tried to gain entry into OTF's office space, and even tried to force OTF's landlord to turn over control of its lease," Gupta said. "This doesn't just harm OTF and the people who work there. OTF works to advance internet freedom in repressive regimes around the world and Pack's actions have put the safety of activists and journalists, in places like Tehran and Hong Kong, at risk."
 "We hope that Pack and the administration will comply with the court of appeals' order," Gupta added.
 The Justice Department declined comment, while a USAGM spokesperson didn’t immediately respond to a request for comment on the new ruling.
title: 上诉法院阻止特朗普任命的人接管网络非营利组织
title_en: Appeals Court Blocks Trump Appointee's Takeover of Web Nonprofit

note:
JAMF股价周三首日大涨高达96%，此前这家帮助企业部署苹果产品的公司IPO定价高于预期区间。
周二，JAMF将1,800万股定价为每股26美元，此前曾将定价区间提高至21美元至23美元。该股在纳斯达克(Nasdaq)攀升至51美元的高点，使该公司市值达到约59亿美元。截至午盘，该股交易价格约为41美元。
这是最新一宗飙升的科技股IPO，表明在冠状病毒(Coronavirus)大流行期间，投资者继续渴望增长，而且企业在上市时仍在桌上留下巨额现金。长期以来，科技公司的首次公开募股(IPO)一直受到批评，因为这一过程允许银行家将定价过低的股票交给新投资者，新投资者往往会立即享受涨势，而发行公司筹集的资金却远远低于其所能筹集的资金。保险科技公司Lemonade本月稍早首日挂牌大涨139%，一周前中国云软件提供商Agora劲扬150%。
成立于2002年的JAMF&39；s说，它的使命是帮助组织与苹果取得成功。苹果是美国市值最高的公司，市值接近1.7万亿美元。
JAMF帮助公司安全地部署Mac电脑、iPhone和iPad，将它们连接在一起，并为IT团队提供管理它们的工具。在招股说明书中，JAMF表示，它有4万名客户部署了超过1700万台苹果设备。
第一季度营收较上年同期增长37%，至6,040万美元，JAMF的毛利率从70%升至75%，原因是更多客户转向其订阅服务。其净亏损从900万美元小幅收窄至830万美元。
JAMF表示，在新冠肺炎疫情爆发期间，随着越来越多的人远程工作，该公司正在减少面对面培训，将更多的课程转移到网上，这正在拉低服务收入。然而，招股说明书显示，越来越多的雇主正在过渡到订阅服务。
该公司表示：我们相信，我们内部的云优先技术平台可以无缝过渡到远程工作环境，而不会对我们的业务产生任何实质性影响，这突显了我们业务模式的弹性。尽管如此，JAMF一再表示，围绕更广泛危机的长度和规模的不确定性是一个重大风险。
另一个风险来自苹果。上个月，这家iPhone制造商宣布对成立四年的公司Fleetsmith进行全面收购，Fleetsmith的软件使远程配置、擦除和部署设备变得更容易。备案文件警告称，JAMF表示，目前它认为Fleetsmith专注于中小企业，但它可以利用这项技术，与我们提供的产品的规模和广度进行更直接的竞争。
JAMF主要由私募股权公司Vista Equity Partners控制，该公司于2017年以7.338亿美元收购了该公司的大部分股份。根据JAMF周三的交易，该公司所持股份目前价值超过45亿美元。
note_en:
JAMF shares soared as much as 96% in their debut on Wednesday after the company, which helps businesses deploy  Apple products, priced its IPO above its expected range.
 On Tuesday, JAMF priced 18 million shares at $26 each, after previously increasing the range to $21 to $23. The stock climbed as high as $51 on the Nasdaq, giving the company a market cap of about $5.9 billion. It was trading around $41 at mid-day.
  It's the latest  tech IPO to soar, showing that investors continue to thirst for growth amid the coronavirus pandemic and that companies are still leaving hefty amounts of cash on the table as they go public. Tech IPOs have long been criticized for a process that lets bankers give underpriced stock to new investors, who often enjoy an immediate pop while the issuing company raises far less money than it could. Insurance tech company  Lemonade jumped 139% in its first day of trading earlier this month, a week after Chinese cloud software provider  Agora rose 150%.
  Founded in 2002, JAMF's says its  mission is to "help organizations succeed with Apple," the most valuable U.S. company with a market cap approaching $1.7 trillion.
 JAMF helps companies securely deploy Mac computers, iPhones and iPads, connecting them together and giving IT teams the tools to manage them. In its  prospectus, JAMF says it has 40,000 customers deploying over 17 million Apple devices.
 First quarter revenue climbed 37% from a year ago, to $60.4 million, and JAMF's gross margin rose to 75% from 70%, as more customers turned to its subscription offering. Its net loss narrowed slightly from $9 million to $8.3 million.
 JAMF said that, with more people working remotely during the Covid-19 outbreak, the company is doing fewer in-person trainings and moving more sessions online, which is pulling down services revenue. However, more employers are transitioning to the subscription service, according to the prospectus.
  "We believe our internal cloud-first technology platforms have allowed for a seamless transition to a remote working environment without any material impacts to our business, highlighting the resilience of our business model," the company said. Still, JAMF repeatedly cites uncertainty surrounding the length and magnitude of the broader crisis as a significant risk.
 Another risk comes from Apple. Last month, the iPhone maker announced the acquisition of  Fleetsmith, a four-year-old company, whose software makes it easier to remotely configure, wipe and deploy devices. JAMF said it currently sees Fleetsmith as focused on small and medium-sized businesses, but it could use the technology "to compete more directly with the scale and breadth of product offerings we provide," the filing warns.
 JAMF is principally controlled by private equity firm Vista Equity Partners, which  acquired a majority of the company in 2017 for $733.8 million. The firm's stake is now worth over $4.5 billion, based on JAMF's trading on Wednesday.
title: 帮助企业部署苹果产品的JAMF将IPO价格定在26美元，高于预期区间，预计IPO首日股价将跃升至51美元，目前交易价格约为37美元
title_en: Jamf, which helps businesses deploy Apple products, prices its IPO above expected range at $26, sees shares jump as high as $51 in debut, now trading at ~$37

周三，俄亥俄州共和党众议员吉姆·乔丹(Jim Jordan)告诉民主党领导层，共和党人呼吁Twitter首席执行官杰克·多尔西(Jack Dorsey)在即将到来的反垄断听证会上作证，听证会将有亚马逊、苹果、Facebook和谷歌的首席执行官参加。
在发出这一呼吁之前，司法委员会的最高共和党人和民主党领导人在听证会形式上关系紧张。
乔丹将Twitter描述为社交媒体市场的领先者，并表示，作为委员会调查的一部分，听证会代表着一次重要而独特的机会，可以探讨与Twitter有关的这些问题。
共和党人要求Twitter首席执行官杰克·多尔西在周一的反垄断听证会上作证，亚马逊、苹果、Facebook和谷歌的首席执行官将参加听证会。
在周三写给民主党领导层的一封信中，俄亥俄州共和党人吉姆·乔丹(Jim Jordan)写道：我们相信，两党都有兴趣听取Twitter关于其在市场上的影响力、其在审查其平台内容方面的作用，以及最近备受关注的安全漏洞的原因，这可能指的是上周影响到包括前总统巴拉克·奥巴马(Barack Obama)在内的主要人物账户的黑客攻击。这封信是写给纽约州民主党司法主席杰罗尔德·纳德勒(Jerrold Nadler)的。
这一呼吁是在司法委员会共和党领袖乔丹和民主党领导人在听证会形式上持续紧张之后发出的。约旦此前要求纳德勒在全体委员会而不是反垄断小组委员会面前举行听证会，以允许所有成员参与。在周三的信中，乔丹说，纳德勒到目前为止已经拒绝了这一请求。
乔丹将Twitter描述为社交媒体市场的领先者，并表示，作为委员会调查的一部分，听证会代表着一次重要而独特的机会，可以探讨与Twitter有关的这些问题。
但Twitter的规模比其他四家已经准备作证的公司小得多，这使得针对该公司的反垄断论点更具挑战性。虽然规模不一定是美国反竞争行为的标志，但Twitter在社交媒体市场的份额远远低于Facebook。上个季度，Twitter公布了1.66亿可货币化的日活跃用户(MDAU)。尽管Facebook表示，这一指标无法与其他平台相比，但远低于Facebook上个季度报告的17.3亿日活跃用户(DAU)。
尽管如此，上周的黑客攻击展示了Twitter凭借其由记者、政客和首席执行官组成的精英用户群所拥有的巨大权力。黑客能够发布虚假消息，从包括前副总统乔·拜登、特斯拉首席执行官埃隆·马斯克和微软创始人比尔·盖茨在内的知名人士的账户中募集加密货币。
包括乔丹在内的保守派人士辩称，Twitter和其他科技公司通过有偏见的算法和不一致的政策应用来审查自己的声音，Twitter否认了这一点。该公司最近甚至成为保守派愤怒的焦点，因为它在唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)总统的推文上贴上了事实核查和警告标签，称其违反了自己的政策。不久之后，特朗普宣布了一项行政命令，呼吁联邦机构考虑围绕法律盾牌制定新规则，以保护在线平台免受其适度做法和用户帖子的责任。
纳德勒的发言人没有立即回复记者的置评请求。Twitter发言人拒绝对这封信发表评论。
本月早些时候，乔丹和反垄断委员会排名靠前的成员、威斯康星州共和党人吉姆·森森布伦纳(Jim Sensenbrenner)写信给多尔西，要求提供有关Twitter内容审查程序的信息，包括它对特朗普推文进行审查的决定。乔丹在最新的一封信中说，多尔西尚未回复那封信，信中要求在周三之前提供材料。
note_en:
Republicans have called on Twitter CEO Jack Dorsey to testify at the upcoming antitrust hearing featuring CEOs of Amazon, Apple, Facebook and Google, Rep. Jim Jordan, R-Ohio, told Democratic leadership on Wednesday.
 The call follows tension between the top Republican on the Judiciary Committee and Democratic leaders of the over the hearing format.
 Jordan describes Twitter as "a market leader in social media" and said the hearing "represents a significant and unique opportunity to explore these issues with respect to Twitter as part of the Committee's investigation."
 Republicans have called on  Twitter CEO Jack Dorsey to testify at  Monday's antitrust hearing, which will feature the CEOs of  Amazon,  Apple,  Facebook and  Google.
 In a letter to  Democratic leadership on Wednesday, Jim Jordan, R-Ohio, wrote, "We believe there is bipartisan interest to hear from Twitter about its power in the marketplace, its role in moderating content on its platform, and the causes for its recent highly publicized security breaches," a likely reference to  last week's hack that affected the accounts of major figures including former President Barack Obama. The letter was addressed to Judiciary Chairman Jerrold Nadler, D-N.Y.
  The call follows  ongoing tension between Jordan, the top Republican on the Judiciary Committee, and Democratic leaders over the format of the hearing. Jordan previously asked Nadler to hold the hearing in front of the full committee, rather than before the Antitrust Subcommittee, to allow participation from all members. In Wednesday's letter, Jordan said that Nadler has so far declined that request.
 Jordan described Twitter as "a market leader in social media" and said the hearing "represents a significant and unique opportunity to explore these issues with respect to Twitter as part of the Committee's investigation."
 But Twitter is much smaller than the four other companies already set to testify, making an antitrust argument against the company more challenging. Though size is not necessarily an indication of anticompetitive behavior in the U.S., Twitter holds far less of the social media market than Facebook. Last quarter, Twitter reported 166 million monetizable daily active users (mDAUs). Though it says that metric is not comparable to those for other platforms, it's far below the 1.73 billion daily active users (DAUs) Facebook reported last quarter.
 Still,  last week's hack demonstrated the amount of power Twitter wields by virtue of its elite userbase comprised of journalists, politicians and CEOs. Hackers were able to post fake messages soliciting cryptocurrency from the accounts of prominent people including former Vice President Joe Biden,  Tesla CEO Elon Musk and  Microsoft founder Bill Gates.
 Conservatives, including Jordan, have argued that Twitter and other tech companies censor their voices through biased algorithms and inconsistent application of policies, which Twitter has denied. The company has recently become even more of a focus of conservative ire after it placed  fact-check and warning labels over tweets by President Donald Trump it claimed had violated its policies. Shortly after, Trump  announced an executive order calling for federal agencies to consider new rules around the  legal shield that protects online platforms from liability for their moderation practices and users' posts.
  A spokesperson for Nadler did not immediately respond to a request for comment. A Twitter spokesperson declined to comment on the letter.
 Jordan and Antitrust Subcommittee Ranking Member Jim Sensenbrenner, R-Wis., wrote Dorsey earlier this month asking for information on Twitter's content moderation processes, including its decisions to moderate tweets by Trump. Jordan said in the latest letter that Dorsey had yet to respond to that letter, which asked for materials by Wednesday.
title: 众议院共和党人呼吁Twitter首席执行官杰克·多尔西(Jack Dorsey)在周一的反垄断听证会上作证，听证会上有亚马逊、苹果、Facebook和谷歌的首席执行官参加
title_en: House Republicans call on Twitter CEO Jack Dorsey to testify at Monday's antitrust hearing featuring CEOs of Amazon, Apple, Facebook, and Google

随着电影似乎无限期中断-为经常推迟的Tenet倒一部-直到我们共同行动起来，流媒体平台正在弥补这一缺口，推出大量原创电影和新剧集，让我们在重新疏远社会的同时保持娱乐。Netflix刚刚删除了最新一部海外发行的预告片：德国科幻惊悚片《生物黑客》，讲述了一名雄心勃勃的年轻医学生为过去的科学罪过向导师寻求报复的故事。
除了节目制作人克里斯蒂安·迪特和主要演员的身份外，目前还没有太多关于这部电视剧的背景信息(甚至连IMDb都没有)。这位出生于德国的迪特在美国最出名的是执导了2016年的浪漫喜剧“如何单身”，以及根据Netflix创始人索菲娅·阿莫鲁索(Sophia Amoruso)的自传改编的2017年Netflix喜剧“女孩老板”(GirlBoss)。(GIRLBOSS收到的评价褒贬不一，一季后被取消。)。生物黑客看起来对他来说是一个重大的转变，考虑到德国科幻系列片“黑暗”(Dark)的成功，这似乎是一个特别有希望的选择。这部电视剧刚刚在Netflix结束了令人费解的第三季，也是最后一季。
杰西卡·施瓦茨(Jessica Schwarz)在美国本土最出名的是她在2006年的惊悚片《香水：杀人犯的故事》(根据帕特里克·苏斯金德1985年的小说改编)中的表演，饰演坦贾·洛伦茨教授(Tanja Lorenz)。瑞士女演员露娜·韦德勒饰演米娅，一名年轻的医科学生，被卷入地下生物黑客和非法基因工程实验的世界。去年秋天，迪特在拍摄结束后告诉Biohackinfo，普通人不得不面对特殊情况的故事一直让我着迷。与Netflix的合作让我们能够讲述一个扣人心弦的故事，聚焦于多层次、可信的人物。
从THC芯片到基因增强，欢迎来到生物黑客的世界。当米娅(露娜·韦德勒饰)开始攻读医学学位时，她似乎和其他任何学生一样。但当她获得才华横溢的洛伦茨教授(杰西卡·施瓦茨饰)的信任后，她显然在隐瞒一个大到可以改变人类命运的大秘密，而来自《女孩老板》(Christian Ditter)导演的这部惊悚片将会让你质疑生活本身的构成。
预告片的开头是洛伦茨教授给她即将到来的班级讲课。她说，让我们来谈谈未来吧。合成生物学已经把我们从生物变成了创造者。它不仅是医学的未来，也是人类的未来。创造未来的世界是我们的责任。你们是明天的创造者。然后，我们看到米娅来到医学院，见到了她的新室友：一个回答门铃的女人，一个看起来很讨人喜欢的书呆子年轻男人，还有另一个自豪地炫耀她的科学项目-一种植物性生物钢琴的年轻女人。
室友们很快就遇到了一对可爱的、有科学头脑的家伙，贾斯珀和尼克拉斯，其中一人邀请米娅到他家参观他家的生物黑客实验室。诚然，这相当于邀请某人去看蚀刻版画，但贾斯珀已经设法给一只老鼠注射了绿色荧光蛋白，让它发出绿色的光，这是量身定做的，很有魅力。
不久之后，贾斯珀就帮助雄心勃勃的米娅在洛伦茨教授的生物技术公司找到了一份工作。而这就是麻烦开始的时候。米娅想要加入洛伦茨的研究小组，询问到底在做什么类型的研究，可能是别有用心的。一位神秘的贾斯帕告诉她，你在大学里不能做的事情。米娅的好奇心引起了洛伦兹的怀疑。米娅似乎确实有一个秘密，与她对弟弟的童年记忆有关。也许他们都是洛伦茨早期实验的一部分，但都以失败告终。米娅发誓要毁了洛伦兹以示报复。但在这个过程中，她最终会把她的新朋友置于危险之中吗？
note_en:
With movies on seemingly indefinite hiatus—pour one out for the  oft-delayed  Tenet—until we collectively get our act together, streaming platforms are picking up the slack, pumping out original films and new series to keep us entertained while we're all social distancing. Netflix just dropped the trailer for its latest foreign offering: the German sci-fi thriller   Biohackers, about an ambitious young medical student seeking revenge on her mentor for the scientific sins of the past.
 There isn't much background information available yet on the series (not even on IMDb), other than the identity of the showrunner,  Christian Ditter, and the main cast members. The German-born Ditter is best known in the United States for directing the 2016 rom-com  How to Be Single and for his work on the 2017 Netflix comedy   Girlboss, based on the autobiography of Sophia Amoruso, who founded the company Nasty Gal. ( Girlboss received mixed reviews and was cancelled after one season.)  Biohackers looks like a significant departure for him and an especially promising one, given the success of the German sci-fi series   Dark, which just wrapped a mind-bending third and final season on Netflix.
 Jessica Schwarz, best known stateside for her performance in the 2006 thriller   Perfume: The Story of a Murderer (based on the 1985 novel by Patrick Susskind), stars as Professor Tanja Lorenz. Swiss actress  Luna Wedler plays Mia, a young medical student who gets drawn into the world of underground biohacking and illegal genetic-engineering experiments. "Stories in which ordinary people have to face exceptional circumstances have always fascinated me,"  Ditter told Biohackinfo last fall after shooting wrapped. "Working with Netflix has allowed us to tell a gripping story that focuses on multi-layered and believable characters."
     From THC chips to genetic enhancements, welcome to the world of Biohacking. When Mia (Luna Wedler) begins her medical degree, she seems like any other student. But when she gains the trust of the brilliant Professor Lorenz (Jessica Schwarz), it becomes apparent that she’s hiding a secret so big it could change the fate of humanity. From the director of  Girlboss (Christian Ditter) comes a thriller that will make you question the makeup of life itself.
 The trailer opens with Professor Lorenz lecturing her incoming class. "Let's talk about the future," she says. "Synthetic biology has turned us from creatures to creators. It's not only the future of medicine, but of humankind. It is our responsibility to create the world of the future. You are the creators of tomorrow." Then we see Mia arriving for med school and meeting her new roommates: a woman who answers the door starkers, an adorably geeky-looking young man, and another young woman who proudly shows off her science project—a plant-based bio-piano.
 The roommates soon meet a couple of cute science-minded guys, Jasper and Niklas, one of whom invites Mia to his place to check out his home biohacking lab. Granted, it's the science-nerd equivalent of inviting someone up to see one's etchings, but Jasper has managed to inject a mouse with green fluorescent protein so it glows green, which is tailor-made to charm.
 It isn't long before Jasper helps land the ambitious Mia a job at Professor Lorenz's biotech company. And that's when the trouble starts. Mia might have ulterior motives for wanting to join Lorenz's research group, asking questions about exactly what kind of research is being done. "Things you can't do at the university," a cryptic Jasper tells her. Mia's curiosity arouses Lorenz's suspicions. And Mia does have a secret, it seems, relating to childhood memories of her younger brother. Maybe they were both part of one of Lorenz's early experiments, which ended badly. Mia vows to ruin Lorenz in revenge. But will she end up putting her new friends in danger in the process?
title: Netflix的德国科幻惊悚片生物黑客可能就是新的黑暗
title_en: Netflix’s German sci-fi thriller Biohackers just might be the new Dark

美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)正在向客户发送电子邮件，警告他们他们的手机很快就会停止工作，他们应该购买一部新手机才能继续获得数据和语音服务。但这封电子邮件具有误导性，因为这些旧手机将继续使用AT&amp；T&T；的网络，直到2022年，或再过18个月。更糟糕的是，AT&amp；T的电子邮件发送给了一些客户，他们的手机拥有在2022年停电后继续工作所需的现代技术。
AT&amp；T的电子邮件称，我们将很快升级我们的网络，以使用最新的技术，正如安卓警察今天发布的一篇文章所显示的那样。&#34；但是您的设备...。与新网络不兼容，您需要继续更换它才能继续接收服务。我们的团队在这里帮助您找到一款符合您需求的兼容设备。这封电子邮件宣传新手机当天免费送货。
电子邮件链接到AT&amp；的支持页面，标题为“做好准备，3G将于2022年消失”。支持页面称AT&amp；T将在2022年2月之前逐步淘汰其3G网络，一旦我们这样做了，不支持HD Voice的3G设备和4G无线设备将不再在我们的网络上工作。&#34；技术支持页面说AT&amp；T将在2022年2月之前逐步淘汰其3G网络，一旦我们这样做，不支持HD Voice的3G设备和4G无线设备将不再在我们的网络上工作。
虽然发给客户的电子邮件链接到该页面，但电子邮件本身让人感觉手机在不久的将来就会停止工作，而不是从现在起18个月后。收到电子邮件但没有点击链接的人可能会认为他们现在必须买一部新手机。
此外，一些客户即使已经拥有支持4G和支持AT&amp；T&T；高清语音服务的VoLTE(VoLTE)技术的手机，也能收到电子邮件。一名怀疑美国电话电报公司的电子邮件是骗局的客户昨天在美国电话电报公司的支持论坛上写道：
我刚刚收到AT&amp；T发来的一封电子邮件，说他们的网络与我的设备不再兼容，我必须升级。首先，这封电子邮件对我来说听起来很可疑。电子邮件地址行中有奇怪的字母和数字。不幸的是，在这种持续流行的情况下，你必须非常小心，什么是真实的，而不是一个骗局。这是合法的还是不合法的？我的手机已经有一年多的历史了，是Android S10E。
该客户提到的三星Galaxy S10E于2019年3月发布，并支持高清语音，AT&amp；自己的网站也证实了这一点。另一位收到这封电子邮件的人在支持论坛上写道，我确认我的手机支持4G和高清语音，这正是AT&amp；所说的需要的。同时支持4G和高清语音的手机比他们列出的要多得多。有人不是懒惰，就是在撒谎。
一位论坛发帖者认为这封电子邮件肯定是个骗局，因为任何一部使用了一年的手机都不会突然不兼容。但这封电子邮件是合法的。在另一个AT&amp；T支持帖子中，一位客户今天询问这封电子邮件是否是骗局，AT&amp；T的一名员工回答说，这不是骗局。美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)正在关闭3G，这样不兼容VoLTE的手机就不能再用了。
我们今天联系了AT&amp；T，如果公司回答了我们的问题，我们将更新这篇文章。
问题显然不在于手机本身是否具备支持高清语音的技术，而在于美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)是否已将其列入白名单。安卓警察写道，AT&amp；T只将其直接销售给客户进行4G语音通话的一小部分手机列入了白名单，而且看起来即使是同样的非运营商手机也不一定能像前面提到的Galaxy S10E那样与运营商的高清语音服务兼容。取决于有多少手机是不兼容的，这封电子邮件可能已经发送给了相当数量的订户。
在美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)的支持论坛上，一位顾客写道，在经济危机期间敦促人们购买新手机是相当失聪的。值得一提的是，许多受影响的手机(比如我的诺基亚6.1)都支持高清语音；AT&amp；T只是没有费心把它们列入白名单。诺基亚6.1使用的是支持LTE语音的高通骁龙630处理器。
AT&amp；T的这个网页列出了在我们的网络上运行的支持高清语音的设备。&#34；Galaxy S10E是其中之一，但诺基亚6.1不是。AT&amp；T网站上还有一份支持HD Voice的解锁手机名单，同样包括三星Galaxy S10E，但不包括诺基亚6.1。
另一个支持帖子是由一位客户发起的，他问为什么AT&amp；T&34；告诉我，我的手机(谷歌Pixel 3A Plus)不再与AT&amp；兼容。AT&amp；T列出的高清语音设备包括Pixel 3A和3A XL，所以这位客户似乎收到了错误的通知。
除了AT&amp；T的电子邮件显然寄给了不应该收到的人之外，这封电子邮件本身也让客户感到困惑。一位客户抱怨说，这封电子邮件的格式很糟糕，基本上没有内容-人们必须通过链接才能获得更多信息。当我愚蠢地试图关注一个链接时，它还是断了，强化了这封电子邮件是假的想法。
打电话给AT&amp；T&34；音盲的客户表示，这一变化沟通得非常糟糕，并向运营商提供了一些常识性建议，他写道：
允许所有支持高清语音的设备使用AT&amp；T网络上的这些功能。

大家好，这是忙碌的一周，TechCrunch的早期阶段正在进行，还有大量的技术收益需要分析。但这并没有阻止Equity摄制组坐下来谈论最近一波SPAC评论。
丹尼和我想谈谈什么是SPAC-这个首字母缩写代表特殊目的收购公司-以及为什么每个人似乎都在和他们搭讪。
昨天，在提高IPO价格区间后，JAMF定价为每股26美元，出售的股票数量超过了此前的预期。
今天，它的开盘价约为48美元，目前每股价值40.18美元，远远高于其IPO价格。
最近的首日上涨，就像JAMF自己的涨幅一样，激怒了风投阶层的一些人，他们认为IPO价格与公司首次交易地点之间的差距是华尔街银行家和IPO过程本身偷来的钱。
进入SPAC，这可能会为独角兽和其他风险支持的公司提供一种通过不同的定价机制上市的方式。如果另一种定价方法对公司来说会更好，目前还不清楚，但我们试图通过谈判来解决。
当然，股市在周五上午回升。请记住，当我提到“罗宾汉笨蛋”时，我指的是所有散户投资者作为一个群体，而不仅仅是某个特定交易平台的人。然而，由于罗宾汉在市场上的声望，我确实在更广泛的范围内将其用作散户投资者的速记。
股市每周一上午7点下跌。PT和周五上午6点。PT，所以订阅我们的苹果播客，阴天，Spotify和所有的演员。
note_en:
Hello everyone, it’s a busy week with  TechCrunch Early Stage underway and a slew of tech earnings to parse through. But that didn’t stop the Equity crew from sitting down to chat about the recent wave of SPAC commentary
 Danny and  I wanted to talk about what a SPAC is — the acronym stands for  special purpose acquisition company — and why everyone seems to be chatting them up.
  Yesterday, after  raising its IPO price range, Jamf  priced at $26 per share, selling more shares than it had previously anticipated.
 Today it opened trading around $48, and is currently worth $40.18 per share, far above its IPO price.
 Recent first-day gains, like Jamf’s own, have peeved  elements of the venture classes who think that the gap between an IPO price and where a company first trades is money that Wall Street bankers, and the IPO process itself, have stolen.
 Enter SPACs, which  could offer a way for unicorns and other venture-backed companies to go public through a different pricing mechanism. If that alternative method of pricing the company would be better is not clear, but we tried to talk it through.
 Equity is back Friday morning, of course. And please bear in mind that when I referred to “ Robinhood   dipshits,” I was talking about  all retail investors as a cohort, not merely the folks at any one particular trading platform. Thanks to the in-market prestige of Robinhood, however, I did use it as short-hand for retail investors more broadly.
  Equity drops every Monday at 7:00 a.m. PT and Friday at 6:00 a.m. PT, so subscribe to us on   Apple Podcasts ,   Overcast ,   Spotify  and all the casts.

note:
这家初创公司是由首席执行官阿卡什·拉朱(Akash Raju)、首席运营官阿努吉·梅塔(Anuj Mehta)和首席运营官库沙尔·内吉(Kushal Negi)创立的，他们都刚刚从普。这是加速器Y Combinator目前一批初创公司的一部分-无论是否巧合，Airbnb都是该公司最著名的校友。
Raju说，他和他的联合创始人在还在上学的时候就想出了这个想法，并与品牌合作在校园里创建快闪商店。他们意识到，许多新的、直接面向消费者的品牌都在寻求提高知名度，他们认为Airbnbs是说服人们尝试(例如)新床垫或新咖啡的完美场所。毕竟，酒店已经在做产品植入业务了。
如果你是Airbnb的主机，你可以注册，然后浏览免费的产品样品。(如果你真的想进货，可以折扣价大量购买。)。
Glimse与您一起展示您酒店上的产品，并通过电子邮件向客人发送电子“Lookbook”，在客人入住开始和结束时突出各种产品。当这些样品引导消费者购买时，你就会赚取佣金(Raju说平均100到500美元，尽管对于大件商品来说可能更高)。
通过该平台进行营销的品牌包括GhostBed床垫和液态死亡水。
这家初创公司于今年3月首次推出-这对旅游业务来说并不是最好的时机。Raju回忆说：“我们实际上是在COVID开始之前发射的。在那之后，我们真正花了很多时间的是对主持人的感同身受。“。
事实上，“瞥见”的一些早期合作伙伴在一段时间内不再挂牌他们的房产。但旅行再次增加，包括(甚至特别是)通过Airbnb，Raju表示，Glimse目前750处房产中的许多现在都预订满了，一直持续到9月份。考虑到过去几个月的收入损失，房东可能会比往常更感兴趣。
他补充说，该团队将继续打造具有新功能的平台，用于产品发现和归因，但他表示，“让我们独一无二的关键是我们对家庭体验的重视。”
note_en:
The startup was founded by CEO Akash Raju, COO Anuj Mehta and CPO Kushal Negi, who all recently graduated from Purdue University. It’s part of the current batch of startups at accelerator  Y Combinator   — where, coincidentally or not, Airbnb is the most famous alum.
 Raju said that he and his co-founders came up with the idea while they were still in school and working with brands to create pop-up shops on campus. They realized that many new, direct-to-consumer brands are looking to increase awareness, and they decided that Airbnbs were the perfect place to convince someone to try (for example) a new mattress or a new kind of coffee. After all, hotels are  already in the product placement business.
  If you’re an Airbnb host, you can sign up and then browse offers for free product samples. (If you really want to stock up, you can buy larger quantities at a discounted price.)
 Glimpse works with you to showcase the products on your properties, and to email a digital “lookbook” highlighting the various products to guests at the beginning and end of their stay. You then earns a commission fee (Raju said $100 to $500 on average, though it can be even higher for big-ticket items) when these samples lead consumers to make a purchase.
  Brands who have marketed themselves through the platform include the GhostBed mattress and Liquid Death water.
 The startup first launched in March of this year — not exactly the best time for the travel business. Raju recalled, “We actually launched right before COVID started. After that, what we really spent a lot of time on was empathizing with hosts.”
 In fact, some of the Glimpse’s early partners stopped listing their properties for a while. But travel is on the rise again, including (or even especially)  via Airbnb, and Raju said many of Glimpse’s 750 current properties are now fully booked through September. And given the lost income of the past few months, hosts might be even more interested than usual.
 He added that the team will continue building out the platform with new features for product discovery and attribution, but he said, “The key thing that makes us unique is our emphasis on that in-home experience.”

美国货币监理署(OCC)允许美国所有全国性注册银行为加密货币提供托管服务。
在一封日期为7月22日的公开信中，高级副主计长兼高级法律顾问乔纳森·古尔德(Jonathan Gould)写道，任何国家银行都可以持有加密货币钱包的唯一加密密钥，为国家银行为客户持有数字资产扫清了道路。
这封信标志着密码行业的一项重大发展。此前，托管是Coinbase等专业公司的专属领域，它们通常需要州许可证(如信托宪章)才能向大型投资者提供服务。现在，已经为股票等提供类似保管服务的大型、受监管的金融公司可能会加入竞争。
这封信似乎写给了一家身份不明的银行或类似的实体，信中指出，银行“可能会提供比现有选择更安全的存储服务”，消费者和投资顾问都可能希望使用受监管的托管人，以确保他们不会丢失私钥，从而不会丢失对资金的访问。
信中说：“为加密货币提供托管在几个方面与其他托管活动有所不同。”
它指出了对数字钱包的需求，并补充说，由于它们存在于区块链上，因此不存在密码的物理占有。
信中说：“OCC认识到，随着金融市场变得越来越科技化，银行和其他服务提供商可能会越来越需要利用新技术和创新方式代表客户提供传统服务。”
它还规定，进入该领域的银行“应开发和实施那些与健全的风险管理实践相一致的活动，并使其与银行的整体业务计划和战略保持一致。”
OCC目前由今年早些时候加入监管机构的Coinbase前高管布莱恩·布鲁克斯(Brian Brooks)领导。自夏季初以来，他一直担任代理审计长，并已经提出了一系列有利于密码公司的改革方案，包括一项国家支付宪章，该宪章将允许密码初创公司在提供支付服务的情况下绕过各州在获得资金传输许可证方面的做法。
周三的信还“重申了OCC的立场，即国家银行可以向它们选择的任何合法业务提供允许的银行服务，包括加密货币业务，只要它们有效地管理风险并遵守适用的法律。”
摩根大通(JPMorgan Chase)就是这样一家为密码公司提供银行服务的全国性银行，今年早些时候曾向双子座和Coinbase提供支持。然而，与国外同行一样，美国的银行通常对为该行业提供服务感到不安，认为交易所和其他初创企业是声誉和合规风险。
CoinDesk是区块链新闻的领先者，是一家努力追求最高新闻标准并遵守一套严格的编辑政策的媒体渠道。CoinDesk是数字货币集团(Digital Currency Group)的独立运营子公司，该集团投资于加密货币和区块链初创公司。
note_en:
The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) is letting all nationally chartered banks in the U.S. provide custody services for cryptocurrencies.
 In a public letter dated July 22, Senior Deputy Comptroller and Senior Counsel Jonathan Gould wrote that any national bank can hold onto the unique cryptographic keys for a cryptocurrency wallet, clearing the way for national banks to hold digital assets for their clients.
 The letter marks a major development for the crypto industry. Previously, custody was the province of specialist firms, such as Coinbase, which typically needed a state license, such as a trust charter, to offer the service to large investors. Now, large, regulated financial companies that already provide similar safekeeping services for stock certificates and the like could enter the fray.
 The letter, which appears to be addressed to an unidentified bank or similar entity, notes that banks “may offer more secure storage services compared to existing options,” and that both consumers and investment advisors may wish to use regulated custodians to ensure they don’t lose their private keys, and therefore, access to their funds.
 “Providing custody for cryptocurrencies would differ in several respects from other custody activities,” the letter said.
 It pointed to the need for digital wallets, adding that because they exist on a blockchain, there is no physical possession for cryptos.
 “The OCC recognizes that, as the financial markets become increasingly technological, there will likely be increasing need for banks and other service providers to leverage new technology and innovative ways to provide traditional services on behalf of customers,” the letter said.
  It also specified that banks entering the space “should develop and implement those activities consistent with sound risk management practices and align them with the bank’s overall business plans and strategies.”
 The OCC is currently headed up by Brian Brooks, a former Coinbase exec who joined the regulator earlier this year. He’s filled in as Acting Comptroller since the beginning of the summer, and has already proposed a number of reforms that would benefit crypto companies, including a national payments charter which would let crypto startups bypass the state-by-state approach in terms of acquiring money transmission licenses if they provide payment services.
 Wednesday’s letter also “reaffirms the OCC’s position that national banks may provide permissible banking services to any lawful business they choose, including cryptocurrency businesses, so long as they effectively manage the risks and comply with applicable law.”
 JPMorgan Chase is one such national bank that provides banking services to crypto companies, having provided support to  Gemini and Coinbase earlier this year. Like their counterparts abroad, however, banks in the U.S. have generally been skittish about serving the industry, perceiving exchanges and other startups as a reputational and compliance risk.
      Disclosure The leader in blockchain news, CoinDesk is a media outlet that strives for the highest journalistic standards and abides by a  strict set of editorial policies. CoinDesk is an independent operating subsidiary of Digital Currency Group, which invests in cryptocurrencies and blockchain startups.
title: 监管机构称，美国银行现在可以提供加密托管服务

note:
西雅图--互联网先驱、亚马逊(Amazon)前副总裁蒂姆·布雷(Tim Bray)在5月初给这家科技巨头带来了冲击波，因为他所说的贯穿该公司文化的“毒脉”辞职了。
在几个小时内，他关于辞职的博客帖子吸引了数十万的浏览量，他的收件箱里挤满了记者、招聘人员和技术人员的请求。很快，国会山的立法者引用了这篇帖子。这一切都让65岁的布雷成为亚马逊最引人注目的叛逃者。
在那之后的几周里，他的脑力不是用来解决编码问题，而是用来对公司提出更广泛的批评。在公司内部引起关注的谈话和博客文章中，他呼吁成立工会和反垄断监管。布雷在一篇帖子中写道，在“反垄断的鼓声中”，他希望看到亚马逊将其零售业务从利润丰厚的云计算部门中分离出来。
“我很确定我不是一个人，”他说。
面对愈演愈烈的反垄断审查，同时冠状病毒危机令公司运营紧张，亚马逊越来越被迫为自己作为雇主的记录以及与消费者的关系进行辩护。周一，该公司首席执行官杰夫·贝佐斯(Jeff Bezos)将首次在国会作证，国会正在调查亚马逊和其他科技巨头的权力。
布雷之所以脱颖而出，是因为虽然对亚马逊的大部分批评都来自外部--劳工团体、立法者和竞争对手--但他在公司高层呆了五年多。
在不列颠哥伦比亚省温哥华停靠的一艘轻微摇晃的小船上进行的一系列视频采访中，布雷直截了当地提出了他的想法，这是一个合乎逻辑的问题。在大流行期间，这艘船一直是他的办公室。
他说：“我不是激进的边缘人，因为我认为21世纪的财富和权力过于集中。”“科技行业是可能被拆分的主要候选行业。”
布雷先生可能有一种独特的倾向，他想的不仅仅是工程问题。他出生于加拿大，大部分时间在贝鲁特长大，他的父亲在那里担任教授。布雷说，由于政治和宗教冲突使黎巴嫩变得不稳定，“它不是一个好的居住地。”
回到加拿大后，他在贝鲁特的时光一直陪伴着他，使他无法忽视政治。他说：“那里的政治采取了非常罕见的街头骚乱和以色列导弹袭击的形式。”
在多伦多附近的圭尔夫大学(University Of Guelph)读书时，布雷先生在计算机科学领域找到了乐趣和技能。他在消费者互联网的早期使用了它，将“牛津英语词典”数字化，并创立了两家初创公司。但他在技术专家中最出名的是帮助发明了XML，这是在互联网上存储和共享数据的关键标准。
到2014年，在谷歌工作了几年之后，布雷加入了亚马逊。他成为了一名罕见的“杰出工程师”，成为精英团队的一员，他们的影响力不是来自于管理大型团队，而是来自于展示工程才华。
保罗·霍夫曼(Paul Hoffman)在本世纪头十年为博客编写技术标准时认识了布雷，他说，布雷是那种你真的想恨但恨不了的人之一，他是一个博学的人，只睡了几个小时就非常能干。
布雷绝对是个“极客”，霍夫曼说，“但不典型的是，他还有很多其他爱好。”
在谈话和写作中，布雷很容易引用经济学家托马斯·皮凯蒂(Thomas Piketty)的话(他“从头到尾”读过他关于不平等的书)，承认喜欢重金属(他称重金属“有点可笑”，因为“音量大得多，超出了正常需要的程度”)，并详细谈论了气候危机(他觉得气候危机令人担忧，“因为他是一个高度尊重定量科学，了解数学建模是什么的人”)。
他将其中一些利益转化为激进主义。2018年，他在抗议加拿大拟议中的一条管道时被捕，该管道将向亚洲市场出口沥青砂石油。去年，当他看到数千名亚马逊员工签署了一封信，敦促亚马逊更有力地解决气候危机时，他加入了自己的名字。他是加入的级别最高的人。
他的参与让组织者兴奋不已。当时帮助组织这封信的亚马逊设计师艾米丽·坎宁安(Emily Cunningham)说，“获得副总裁证明了亚马逊员工对亚马逊在气候问题上发挥重要领导作用的强烈感受。”
布雷说，他的公开异议激怒了亚马逊的一些领导人。他说，他被告知要记住亚马逊的领导原则，即“不同意并承诺”，即人们应该在内部积极辩论，但一旦就一个问题做出正式决定，每个人都应该一致支持。
布雷说，“作为一名副总裁，你不应该因为相互矛盾的信息而脱轨，这并不是一个不合理的立场。”但这个想法最终会导致他辞职。
今年4月，亚马逊解雇了坎宁安和其他几名员工，他们对亚马逊仓库的安全提出了担忧。该公司表示，每名员工都多次违反各种政策。在布雷看来，这看起来“像是一项明确的政策，任何举手的人都会被解雇。”
亚马逊发言人杰西·安德森(Jaci Anderson)在一份声明中说，“我们支持每个员工批评雇主工作条件的权利，但这并不意味着对任何和所有内部政策都有豁免权。”
对布雷来说，解雇是越界的。他说，他在内部提出了担忧，但不能像亚马逊希望的那样“不同意并承诺”。他留了几周来完成一个项目，然后辞职，留下了100万美元的补偿。
他在他的博客上公开解释了辞职的原因。布雷熬夜到凌晨2点，为他的服务器做好准备，如果Reddit和黑客新闻像他希望的那样接收到他的帖子，他的服务器就可以承受比往常更大的流量。这项计划的效果比他预想的还要好。
在接下来的几天里，布雷的批评在华盛顿特区引起了共鸣。他采访了民主党众议员普拉米拉·贾亚帕尔(Pramila Jayapal)，他所在的选区包括亚马逊在西雅图的总部。一群参议员在写给贝佐斯关于解雇的信时提到了他的辞职博客帖子。
他最初试图保持低调，只通过电子邮件回应媒体的请求。但他一直在写博客，并最终公开发表讲话，对该公司提出了更激进的批评。
6月初，在加拿大调查新闻网站“国家观察报”(National Watch)的一段直播视频中，布雷表示，亚马逊是集中资本主义的症状。“我们真的没有亚马逊的问题，”他说。“我们面临的是一个深层次的社会问题，权力和财富的失衡令人无法接受。”
他在活动上表示：“我不清楚为什么零售公司、制造公司、语音识别公司、云计算公司和Prime视频公司应该是同一家公司。”“它们之间没有特别的关联，我认为这是积极的扭曲和有害的。”
一周后，布雷在一个由批评亚马逊的全球工会召开的虚拟会议上发表了讲话。他说，美国的工会应该更容易成立，“我们可以运行的最有力的政治计划之一，目的是纠正我们担心的权力失衡，那就是反垄断。”他还表示，亚马逊和其他大公司的绝对规模赋予了他们对政治、政策和劳动条件的过度权力。
他说，亚马逊所崇尚的顾客“善良”--低价、无穷无尽的选择、快速的送货--“不是免费的”。“目前，所有这些好处的负面影响，绝大多数都是由仓库工人经历的。”
亚马逊为其劳动条件进行了强有力的辩护，称其已花费数十亿美元来确保仓库安全，其员工的时薪至少为15美元，外加福利。
布雷很快就开始阐述拆分公司的商业理由。他用亚马逊的标准格式(称为PRFAQ)撰写了这份报告，设想了一旦提案完全通过，该公司将如何宣布该提案。他写道，随着反垄断压力的增加，亚马逊可能更愿意“主动”剥离其云计算业务亚马逊网络服务(Amazon Web Services)，“而不是在华盛顿的敌意压力下”。
他将这份文件发布在编码协作工具GitHub上，寻求帮助提高音调。他认为，通过剥离AWS，像沃尔玛这样与亚马逊竞争的公司将更放心地使用云计算服务，打开更多潜在客户。
他写道：“与亚马逊竞争的组织希望利用AWS行业领先的产品，而不必担心它们会增强竞争对手的实力。”
他的帖子并没有像他的辞职那样在网上疯传。但查看他博客服务器上的日志，他可以看出这在关键的地方引起了关注：亚马逊内部。
当一位前同事告诉他，亚马逊担心一名华尔街分析师可能会认为这份文件是真正的亚马逊文件时，他的怀疑得到了证实。他可否加上免责声明？
布雷先生更新了提案。“这份文件不是亚马逊的作品，”他写道。“它描述了一个可能发生的假设过程。”
note_en:
SEATTLE — Tim Bray, an internet pioneer and a former vice president at Amazon, sent shock waves through the tech giant in early May when he resigned for what he called “a vein of toxicity” running through its culture.
 Within a few hours,  his blog post about the resignation drew hundreds of thousands of views, and his inbox filled up with requests from journalists, recruiters and techies. Soon, lawmakers on Capitol Hill cited the post. It all made Mr. Bray, 65, Amazon’s highest-profile defector.
  In the weeks since, he has aimed his brain power not at fixing a coding problem but at framing a broader critique of the company. In talks and blog posts that have drawn attention inside the company, he has called for unionization and antitrust regulation. Amid “the beating of the antitrust drums,” Mr. Bray  wrote in one post, he would like to see Amazon separate its retail business from its lucrative cloud computing unit.
 “And I’m pretty sure I’m not alone,” he said.
 Facing growing antitrust scrutiny at the same time that the  coronavirus crisis has strained the company’s operations, Amazon is increasingly forced to defend its record as an employer and its relationship with consumers. On Monday, Jeff Bezos, the company’s chief executive, will testify for the first time before Congress, which is investigating the power of Amazon and other tech titans.
 Mr. Bray stands out because while much of the criticism of Amazon has been from the outside — labor groups, lawmakers and rivals — he spent more than five years in the top echelons of the company.
  In a series of video interviews from a gently rocking small boat, docked in Vancouver, British Columbia, that has been his office during the pandemic, Mr. Bray straightforwardly presented his ideas as a matter of logic.
 “I am not in some radical fringe because I think the wealth and power in the 21st century is overly concentrated,” he said. “The tech industry is a leading candidate for what could be broken up.”
  Mr. Bray may have been uniquely predisposed to think about more than engineering problems. Born in Canada, he grew up largely in Beirut, where his father worked as a professor. As political and religious conflict made Lebanon unstable, “it just wasn’t a good place to live,” Mr. Bray said.
 His time in Beirut stayed with him after he returned to Canada, making him unable to ignore politics. “Politics there takes the very rare form of riots in the streets and incoming Israeli missiles,” he said.
 While a student at University of Guelph, near Toronto, Mr. Bray found joy and skill in computer science. He used it during the early days of the consumer internet, digitizing the Oxford English Dictionary and founding two start-ups. But he is best known among technologists for helping invent XML, a  critical standard for storing and sharing data on the internet.
 By 2014, after several years at Google, Mr. Bray had joined Amazon. He became a rare “distinguished engineer,” part of an elite group whose clout comes not from managing large teams but from demonstrating engineering brilliance.
 Paul Hoffman, who met Mr. Bray in the 2000s while writing technical standards for blogs, said Mr. Bray was one of those people you really want to hate but can’t, a polymath who was highly functional on just a few hours of sleep.
 Mr. Bray is definitely “a geeky geek,” Mr. Hoffman said, “but what is atypical is that he also has a lot of other interests.”
 In conversation and his writing, Mr. Bray readily cites the economist Thomas Piketty (whose book on inequality he has read “end to end”), admits a love of heavy metal (which he calls “sort of, well, ridiculous,” because “the volume is much louder than can be sanely necessary”) and talks in detail about the climate crisis (which he finds alarming “as a person who has a high respect for quantitative science and understands what mathematical modeling is about”).
 He turned some of those interests into activism. In 2018, he was arrested while protesting a proposed pipeline in Canada that would export tar sands oil to Asian markets. And last year, when he saw that  thousands of corporate Amazon employees had signed a letter urging Amazon to address the climate crisis more forcefully, he added his name. He was the most senior person to join.
  His involvement thrilled organizers. “To have a V.P. just confirmed how strongly Amazon employees felt about Amazon taking significant leadership on climate,” said Emily Cunningham, an Amazon designer at the time who helped organize the letter.
 His public dissent angered some leaders at Amazon, Mr. Bray said. He said he had been told to remember the Amazon leadership principle known as “disagree and commit,” the idea that people should vigorously debate internally but that once a formal decision on an issue is made, everyone should fall in line and support it.
 “As a V.P., you’re not supposed to go off the rails with conflicting messaging, which is not an unreasonable position,” Mr. Bray said. But that idea would eventually lead to his resignation.
 In April, Amazon  fired Ms. Cunningham and several  other workers who had raised concerns about safety in Amazon’s warehouses. The company said each employee had repeatedly violated various policies. To Mr. Bray, it looked “like an explicit policy of firing anybody who put up their hand.”
 “We support every employee’s right to criticize their employer’s working conditions, but that does not come with blanket immunity against any and all internal policies,” Jaci Anderson, an Amazon spokeswoman, said in a statement.
 For Mr. Bray, the firings crossed a line. He said he had raised concerns internally but could not “disagree and commit,” as Amazon wanted. He stayed for a few weeks to wrap up a project and resigned, leaving $1 million in compensation behind.
 He turned to his blog to explain the resignation publicly. Mr. Bray stayed up until 2 a.m., preparing his server to withstand greater-than-usual traffic, should Reddit and Hacker News pick up his post, as he hoped. The plan worked even better than he had expected.
  In the days that followed, Mr. Bray’s critique resonated in Washington, D.C. He spoke with Representative Pramila Jayapal,  a Democrat whose district includes Amazon’s headquarters in Seattle. And a group of senators mentioned his resignation blog post when  they wrote to Mr. Bezos about the firings.
 He initially tried to keep a low profile, responding only by email to press requests. But he kept blogging and eventually talked publicly, making even more aggressive criticisms of the company.
 On a live video in early June with National Observer, a Canadian investigative news site, Mr. Bray said Amazon was a symptom of concentrated capitalism. “We don’t really have an Amazon problem,” he said. “What we have is a deep, societal problem with an unacceptable imbalance of power and wealth.”
 “It’s not obvious to me why the retail company, the manufacturing company, the voice recognition company, the cloud computing company and the Prime video company should be the same company,” he  said at the event. “They’re not particularly related to each other, and I think it’s actively distorting and harmful.”
 A week later, Mr. Bray spoke at a virtual conference convened by global unions critical of Amazon. He said that unions should be easier to form in the United States and that “one of the most powerful political programs we could run with the aim of correcting the power imbalances that concern us is antimonopoly.” He also said the sheer size of Amazon and other large corporations gave them inordinate power over politics, policies and labor conditions.
 The “goodness” Amazon espouses for customers — low prices, endless selection, quick delivery — “isn’t free,” he said. “Right now, the downside of all this goodness is overwhelmingly being experienced by the warehouse workers.”
 Amazon has strongly defended its labor conditions, saying that it  has spent billions of dollars to make its warehouses safe and that its workers are paid at least $15 an hour, plus benefits.
  Mr. Bray soon turned to formulating a business case for breaking up the company. He wrote it in a standard Amazon format, known as a PRFAQ, envisioning how the company would announce the proposal once it was fully enacted. With antitrust pressures growing, Amazon might prefer to “proactively” spin off its cloud computing business, Amazon Web Services, he wrote, “as opposed to under hostile pressure from Washington.”
 He posted the document on GitHub, a coding collaboration tool,  asking for help improving the pitch. By spinning AWS off, he argued, companies like Walmart that compete with Amazon would be more comfortable using the cloud computing service, opening up more potential customers.
 “Organizations who compete with Amazon want to take advantage of AWS’s industry-leading offerings without having to worry that they are strengthening a competitor,” he wrote.
 His post did not go viral like his resignation. But looking at logs on his blog’s server, he could tell that it got attention somewhere critical: inside Amazon.
 His suspicion was confirmed when a former colleague told him that Amazon was worried a Wall Street analyst might think the document was a true Amazon document. Could he add a disclaimer?
 Mr. Bray updated the proposal. “This document is not an Amazon production,” he wrote. “It describes a hypothetical process that could take place.”
title: 亚马逊(Amazon)前副总裁蒂姆·布雷(Tim Bray)的个人资料，他于5月份辞职，批评亚马逊的“有毒静脉”，他正在为拆分公司建立一个商业案例
title_en: Profile of Tim Bray, the former VP at Amazon who quit in May criticizing its “vein of toxicity” and who is building a business case for breaking up the company

note:
长期以来，完全同态加密一直被认为是密码学的圣杯之一。完全同态加密(FHE)的承诺是强大的：它是一种加密类型，允许第三方对加密的数据执行计算，并获得加密的结果，他们可以将该加密结果交还给拥有原始数据的解密密钥的任何人，而不需要第三方能够自己解密数据或结果。
举个简单的例子，假设您有一组电子邮件，并且您想要使用第三方垃圾邮件过滤器来检查它们是否是垃圾邮件。垃圾邮件过滤器希望其算法具有私密性：要么垃圾邮件过滤器提供商希望关闭其源代码，要么垃圾邮件过滤器依赖于他们不想公开的非常大的数据库，因为这会使攻击变得更容易，或者两者兼而有之。但是，您关心的是数据的隐私，并且不想将未加密的电子邮件上传给第三方。所以这里是你如何做到这一点的：
完全同态加密有很多应用，包括在区块链领域。一个关键示例是可用于实现隐私保护的轻客户端(轻客户端向服务器提供加密索引i，服务器计算并返回data[0]*(i=0)+data[1]*(i=1)+...+data[n]*(i=n)，其中data[i]是块或状态中的第i条数据及其Merkle分支，并且(i=k)是如果i=k则返回1的表达式；轻客户端获得其需要的数据，并且服务器对轻客户端要求的内容一无所知)。
更高效的隐形寻址协议，以及更普遍的隐私保护协议的可扩展性解决方案，这些协议现在需要每个用户亲自扫描整个区块链以查找传入交易。
保护隐私的数据共享市场，允许用户对其数据执行某些特定计算，同时保持对其数据的完全控制。
更强大的加密原语中的一个组成部分，例如更有效的多方计算协议，也许最终还会进行模糊处理。
事实证明，从概念上讲，完全同态加密并不那么难理解！
首先，关于定义的说明。同态加密有不同的种类，有些比另一些更强大，它们通过人们可以对加密数据进行计算的函数来分隔。
部分同态加密只允许对加密数据进行非常有限的操作集评估：或者只计算加法(因此，给定ENCRYPT(A)和ENCRYPT(B)，您可以计算ENCRYPT(a+b))，或者只计算乘法(给定ENCRYPT(A)和ENCRYPT(B)，您可以计算ENCRYPT(a*b))。
某种程度上的同态加密允许计算加法以及有限数量的乘法(或者，多项式直到有限的程度)。也就是说，如果您得到ENCRYPT(X1)...。ENCRYPT(Xn)(假设这些是原始加密，并且还不是同态计算的结果)，您可以计算ENCRYPT(p(x1...。Xn))，只要p(x1…。Xn)是对某一特定度界D具有&lt；D次的多项式(D通常很低，认为是5-15)。
完全同态加密允许无限的加法和乘法。加法和乘法允许您复制任何二进制电路门(AND(x，y)=x*y，OR(x，y)=x+y-x*y，XOR(x，y)=x+y-2*x*y或只复制x+y，如果您只关心偶数和奇数，而不是(X)=1-x...)，因此这足以对加密数据进行任意计算。
部分同态加密相当容易；RSA具有乘法同态：\(enc(X)=x^e\)，\(enc(Y)=y^e\)，so\(enc(X)*enc(Y)=(Xy)^e=enc(Xy)\)。椭圆曲线可以通过加法提供类似的性质。事实证明，同时允许加法和乘法要困难得多。
这里，我们将介绍一种有点同态的加密算法(即，支持有限数量乘法的人)，这非常简单。克雷格·金特里(Craig Gentry)在2009年使用了这类技术的一个更复杂的版本来创建有史以来第一个完全同态方案。最近的努力已经转向使用基于向量和矩阵的不同方案，但我们仍将首先介绍这项技术。
我们将所有这些加密方案描述为密钥方案；也就是说，使用相同的密钥进行加密和解密。任何私钥HE方案都可以很容易地转变为公钥方案：公钥通常只是一组多个0的加密，以及1的加密(可能还有更多的2的幂)。要加密值，请将非零加密的适当子集加在一起来生成它，然后将零加密的随机子集添加到密文的随机化中，并使其无法辨别其所代表的内容。
这里的密钥是一个大素数\(p\)(可以认为\(p\)有数百甚至数千位数字)。该方案只能加密0或1，并且&#34；加法&#34；变成XOR，即。1+1=0。要加密值\(m\)(0或1)，请生成一个较大的随机值\(R\)(通常比\(p\)还要大)和一个较小的随机值\(r\)(通常比\(p\)小得多)，然后输出：
要添加两个密文\(ct_1\)和\(ct_2\)，只需添加它们：\(ct_1+ct_2\)。要把两个密文相乘，你再一次...。将它们相乘：\(ct_1*ct_2\)。我们可以证明同态性质(加密的和是该和的加密，对于乘积也是如此)，如下所示。
\[ct_1=R_1*p+r_1*2+m_1\]\[ct_2=R_2*p+r_2*2+m_2\]。
\[ct_1+ct_2=R_1*p+R_2*p+r_1*2+r_2*2+m_1+m_2\]。
\[(r_1+R_2)*p+(r_1+r_2)*2+(m_1+m_2)\]。
其形式与密文\(m_1+m_2\)完全相同。如果您解密它，第一个\(mod\p\)删除第一个项，第二个\(mod\2\)删除第二个项，剩下的是\(m_1+m_2\)(请记住，如果\(m_1=1\)和\(m_2=1\)，则2将被吸收到第二个项中，而您将只剩下零)。所以，瞧，我们有加法同态！
\[ct_1*ct_2=(R_1*p+r_1*2+m_1)*(R_2*p+r_2*2+m_2)\]。
\[(r_1*R_2*p+r_1*2+m_1+r_2*2+m_2)*p+\\(r_1*r_2*2+r_1*m_2+r_2*m_1)*2+\\(m_1*m_2)\]。
这只是展开上面的产品，并将包含\(p\)的所有术语组合在一起，然后是包含\(2\)的所有剩余术语，最后是消息的产物。如果您解密，则\(mod\p\)会再次删除第一个组，\(mod\2\)会删除第二个组，只剩下\(m_1*m_2\)。
但是这里有两个问题：第一，密文本身的大小增加了(乘法时长度大约翻了一番)，第二，在较小的\(*2\)项中的噪声#34；(通常也称为##34；错误#34；)也变得平方增大。有必要将此错误添加到密文中，因为该方案的安全性基于近似的GCD问题：
如果我们转而使用精确的GCD问题，打破这个系统将会很容易：如果你只有一组形式为\(p*R_1+m_1\)，\(p*R_2+m_2\)...的表达式，那么你可以使用欧几里得算法来有效地计算最大公约数\(p\)。但是，如果密文只是\(p\)的近似倍数，并且有一定的误差，那么提取\(p\)很快就变得不切实际，因此该方案是安全的。
不幸的是，该错误引入了固有的限制，即如果将密文彼此相乘足够多次，错误最终会增长到足以超过\(p\)，并且在这一点上，\(mod\p\)和\(mod2\)步骤相互干扰，使得数据无法提取。这将是所有这些同态加密方案的固有权衡：从有错误的近似方程式中提取信息比从精确方程式中提取信息困难得多，但是当您对加密数据进行计算时，添加的任何错误都会迅速增加，从而限制了在错误变得不可抗拒之前可以进行的计算量。这就是为什么这些方案只有几分同态的原因。
这个问题有两种解决方案。首先，在许多有些同态的加密方案中，有一些巧妙的技巧可以使乘法只增加一个恒定因子的误差(例如，1000倍)，而不是平方。将误差增加1000倍听起来仍然很多，但请记住，如果\(p\)(或其在其他方案中的等价物)是300位数字，这意味着您可以将数字彼此相乘100倍，这足以计算非常广泛的计算类别。其次，还有克雷格·金特里的自举技巧。
假设您有一个密文\(ct\)，它是密钥\(p\)下的某个\(m\)的加密，这有很多错误。其想法是，我们通过将密文转换为另一个密钥\(p_2\)下的\(m\)新密文来刷新密文，其中该过程清除旧错误(尽管它将引入固定数量的新错误)。这个把戏很巧妙。\(p\)和\(p_2\)的持有者提供引导密钥&#34；，该密钥由密钥\(p_2\)下的\(p\)位的加密以及\(p_2\)的公钥组成。然后，对以\(p\)加密的数据进行计算的人将获取密文\(ct\)的位，并在\(p_2\)下单独加密这些位。然后使用这些密文在\(p\)下同态计算解密，并得到单个比特，该比特将在\(p_2\)下进行\(m\)加密。
这是很难理解的，所以我们可以这样再说一遍。解密过程\(dec(ct，p)\)本身是一种计算，因此它本身可以被实现为将\(ct\)的位和\(p\)的位作为输入，并输出解密的位\({0，1}中的m\)的电路。如果某人有一个在\(p\)下加密的密文\(ct\)，一个\(p_2\)下的公钥，\(p\)在\(p_2\)下加密的比特，那么他们可以同态计算\(dec(ct，p)=m\)&#34；，然后得到在\(p2\)下加密的\(m\)。请注意，解密过程本身会清除旧错误；它只输出0或1。解密过程本身是一个包含加法或乘法的电路，因此会引入新错误，但此新错误并不取决于原始加密中的错误数量。
但是.。这里有个圈套。在如上所述的方案中(使用循环安全或不使用循环安全)，错误爆炸得如此之快，以至于即使是方案本身的解密电路也难以承受。也就是说，在\(p_2\)下加密的新\(m\)已经有太大的错误，以至于它不可读。这是因为每个与门都会使错误的位长翻倍，所以使用d位模数(P)的方案只能处理少于(log(D))次乘法(串联)，但解密需要在由这些二进制逻辑门组成的电路中计算\(mod\p)，这就需要在一个由这些二进制逻辑门组成的电路中计算\(mod\p)。超过\(log(D)\)次乘法。
Craig Gentry想出了一些巧妙的方法来解决这个问题，但可以说这些方法太复杂了，无法解释；相反，我们将直接跳到2011和2013年的更新工作中，以不同的方式解决这个问题。
为了更进一步，我们将介绍Brakerski和Vaikuntanathan在2011年引入的一种不同类型的某种程度上的同态加密，并展示如何引导它。这里，我们将不再将密钥和密文作为整数，而是将密钥和密文作为矢量。给定密钥\(k={k_1，k_2....。K_n}\)，要加密消息\(m\)，需要构造一个向量\(c={c_1，c_2...。C_n}\)使得内积(或点积)\(&lt；c，k&gt；=c_1k_1+c_2k_1+...+c_nk_n\)模为某一固定
在本例中，我们设置了模数\(p=103\)。点积为3*2+14*71+15*82+92*81+65*8=10202，(10202=99*103+5)。5本身当然是\(2*2+1\)，所以消息是1。请注意，在实践中，密钥的第一个元素通常设置为\(1\)；这使得为特定值生成密文变得更容易(看看是否能找出原因)。
该方案的安全性是基于一个被称为错误学习(LWE)的假设，或者，用更专业但也更容易理解的术语来说，就是求解有错误的方程组的难度。
密文本身可以看作一个等式：\(K_1c_1+...。+k_nc_n\约0\)，其中键\(k_1...。K_n\)是未知数，密文\(c_1…。C_n\)是系数，由于消息(0或1)和误差(对于某些相对较小的\(e\)而言\(2e\))，等式只是近似的。LWE假设确保即使您可以访问许多这样的密文，也无法恢复\(k\)。
很容易验证加密是可加性的：如果\(&lt；ct_1，k&gt；=2e_1+m_1\)和\(&lt；ct_2，k&gt；=2e_2+m_2\)，则\(&lt；ct_1+ct_2，k&gt；=2(e_1+e_2)+m_1+m_2\)(这里的加法是模\(。更难的是乘法：与数字不同，没有自然的方法将两个长度为n的向量相乘为另一个长度为n的向量。我们能做的最好的是外积：一个包含每个可能对的乘积的向量，其中第一个元素来自第一个向量，第二个元素来自第二个向量。也就是说，\(a\o乘b=a_1b_1+a_2b_1+...+a_nb_1+a_1b_2+...+a_nb_2+...+a_nb_n\)。我们可以使用方便的数学恒等式(&lt；a\otime b，c\oTimes d&gt；=&lt；a，c&lt；*&lt；b，d&gt；\)将密文相乘。
给定两个密文\(c_1\)和\(c_2\)，我们计算\(c_1\o乘以c_2\)的外积。如果\(c_1\)和\(c_2\)都用\(k\)加密，则\(&lt；c_1，k&gt；=2e_1+m_1\)和\(&lt；c_2，k&gt；=2e_2+m_2\)。外部积\(c_1\ox c_2\)可以看作是\(k\o次k\)下的\(m_1*m_2\)的加密；我们可以通过查看尝试用\(k\o次k\)解密时会发生什么来看到这一点：
\[&lt；c_1\o倍c_2，k\o倍k&gt；\]\[=&lt；c_1，k&gt；*&lt；c_2，k&gt；\]\[=(2e_1+m_1)*(2e_2+m_2)\]\[=2(e_1m_2+e_2m_1+2e_1e_2)+m_1m_2\。
因此，这种外部产品的方法是有效的。但是，正如您可能已经注意到的，这其中有一个问题：密文和密钥的大小呈二次曲线增长。
我们用重新线性化的程序来解决这个问题。私钥的持有者提供作为公钥的一部分的重新线性化密钥，您可以将其视为\(k\)下的\(k\oox k\)加密。其想法是，我们将这些加密的\(k\ox k\)片段提供给执行计算的任何人，允许他们计算公式\(&lt；c_1\ox c_2，k\ox k&gt；\)以解密密文，但只能以这样的方式返回，即输出在\(k\)下加密返回。
重要的是要理解我们这里所说的嘈杂加密是什么意思。通常，该加密方案只允许加密\(m\in\{0，1\})，而\(m\)&#34；的加密是一个向量\(c\)，使得对于某些小误差\(E)，\(&lt；c，k&gt；=m+2e\)是\(&lt；c，k&gt；=m+2e\)。在这里，我们重新加密任意的\(m\in\{0，1，2...p-1\}\)。请注意，该错误意味着您无法从\(c\)；完全恢复\(m\)；您的答案将偏离2的某个倍数。但是，事实证明，对于此特定用例，这是可以的。
重新线性化关键字由一组向量组成，当这些向量与关键字\(k\)内积(模\(p\))时，给出\(ki*k_j*2^d+2e\)形式的值(mod\(p\))，每个可能的三元\((i，j，d)\)对应一个这样的向量，其中\(i\)和\(j\)是关键字中的索引，而\(d\)是指数，其中\(2。P\)(注意：如果密钥的长度为\(n\)，则重新本地化密钥中将有\(n^2*log(P)\)值；请确保在继续之前了解原因)。
现在，让我们退后一步，再看看我们的目标。我们有一个密文，如果用\(k\ox k\)解密，得到\(m_1*m_2\)。我们需要一个密文，如果用\(k\)解密，得到\(m_1*m_2\)。我们可以用重新线性化密钥来做这件事。注意，解密方程\(&lt；ct_1\o乘ct_2，k\o乘k&gt；\)只是形式为\((ct_{1_i}*ct_{2_j})*k_p*k_q\)的项的大和。
我们的再线性化钥匙里有什么？一串\(2^d*k_p*k_q\)形式的元素，在\(k\)下进行噪声加密，表示\(p\)和\(q\)的每种可能组合！在我们的重新线性化密钥中拥有所有2的幂允许我们只需将2的幂\(\le log(P)\)相加即可生成任何\((ct_{1_i}*ct_{2_j})*k_p*k_q\)。13=8+4+1)加在一起表示每个\((p，q)\)对。
例如，如果\(ct_1=[1，2]\)和\(ct_2=[3，4]\)，则\(ct_1\o乘ct_2=[3，4，6，8]\)，且\(enc(&lt；ct_1\o乘ct_2，k\o倍k&gt；)=enc(3k_1k_1+4k_1k_2+6k_2k_1。
\(\color{green}{enc(k_1*k_1)+enc(k_1*k_1*2)}+\color{red}{enc(k_1*k_2*4)}+\)\(\color{Blue}{enc(k_2*k_1*2)+enc(k_2*k_1*4)}+\color{橙色}{enc(k_2*k_2*8)}\)。
注意，在重新线性化密钥中的每个噪声加密都有一些偶数误差\(2e\)，并且等式\(&lt；ct_1\o×ct_2，k\o倍k&gt；\)本身也有一些误差：如果\(&lt；ct_1，k&gt；=2e_1+m_1\)和\(&lt；ct_2+k&gt；=2e_2+m_2\)，则。Ct_1，k&gt；*&lt；ct_2+k&gt；=2(2e_1e_2+e_1m_2+e_2m_1)+m_1m_2。但是这个总误差仍然(相对)小(\(2e_1e_2+e_1m_2+e_2m_1\)加上来自实现密钥的\(n^2*log(P)\)个固定大小的误差)，并且误差是偶数的，所以该计算的结果仍然给出一个值，当与\(k\)内积时，对于某些&#34；组合误差给出了\(m_1*m_2+2e&#39；\)的值，所以该计算结果仍然是(相对)小的(2e_1e_2+e_1m_2+e_2m_1\)加上来自实现密钥的固定大小误差\(n^2*log(P)\)，并且该误差是偶数。
我们在这里使用的更广泛的技术是同态加密中的一个常见技巧：提供在密钥本身下加密的密钥片段(如果您刻意避免循环安全假设，则提供一个不同的密钥)，以便对数据进行计算的人可以计算解密等式，但只能以输出本身仍然加密的方式进行计算。它在上面的引导过程中使用过，在这里也使用过；最好确保您在头脑中理解这两种情况下发生的事情。
